_comment: sovereign-os global-history dashboard (R487, E11.M5+). Visualizes the operator-§1g 'what changed on this system in the last N hours/days?' surface: 6-source delta/differential aggregator (apt / dpkg / shell / osctl / events / modules). Sourced from sovereign_os_operator_global_history_query_total{verb,source,result} (Layer B, SDD-016) emitted by scripts/operator/global-history.py. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Closes the global-history surface-map FUTURE waiver 'dashboard: FUTURE — Grafana timeline panel'.

panels.title: recent verb (count)
panels.description: Chronological recent-events view — operator's primary 'what just happened?' verb.

panels.title: summary verb (count)
panels.description: Per-source count + last-event timestamp — operator's 'is each source live?' verb.

panels.title: delta verb (count)
panels.description: Operator-§1g 'delta / differential' verb — the literal verbatim phrase from §1g.

panels.title: tail verb (count)
panels.description: R481 refresh-loop TUI invocations. Long-running tail sessions show up here.

panels.title: global-history query rate per verb (5m)
panels.description: Per-verb query rate over time. recent / summary / sources / delta / tail — operator-discoverable churn pattern; the area-under spike on `tail` tracks long-watch sessions.

panels.title: global-history per-source attention (5m)
panels.description: Per-source query rate. apt / dpkg / shell / osctl / events / modules / all (joined or aggregate). Tracks which source the operator is asking about — operator's revealed-preference signal.

panels.title: global-history source distribution (cumulative)
panels.description: Cumulative per-source query count. Operator-§1g 6-source ladder visibility: apt / dpkg / shell / osctl / events / modules.

panels.title: global-history current state (verb × source)
panels.description: Per-(verb, source) histogram: which source the operator queries against which verb. Drives the operator's 'am I reading the source that actually answers my question?' visibility.

options.content:
**Operator §1g verbatim** (R448, E11.M5):

> "Some kind of global history too. tracking things happening, delta, differentials... apt changes and operations, or any cli or tool call I guess, in the management. more reliable and adapted than simply aggregating the .bash_history's."

**6-source registry (the operator-discoverable surface):**

1. `apt` — `/var/log/apt/history.log` (install/remove/upgrade with timestamps + initiator user)
2. `dpkg` — `/var/log/dpkg.log` (lower-level package state changes)
3. `shell` — `~/.bash_history` (with timestamps when `HISTTIMEFORMAT` was set during writes; falls back to file-mtime otherwise)
4. `osctl` — `~/.sovereign-os/history/*.jsonl` (every `sovereign-osctl` invocation, when the per-call logger is shipped)
5. `events` — cross-cutting JSONL state files (notify-events, models-eval, fine-tune — same sources `osctl events` reads)
6. `modules` — selfdef + sovereign-os module install/uninstall events (when the selfdef event-log surface ships)

**Distinct from** (operator-discoverable disambiguation):

- `sovereign-osctl history` — BUILD pipeline run-log viewer
- `sovereign-osctl events` — JSONL state-file aggregator (Layer A cross-cutting)
- `sovereign-osctl journal` — systemd-journal viewer (Layer B)

**The §1g promise:** `more reliable and adapted than simply aggregating the .bash_history's` — the 6-source ladder fulfills that contract by reading authoritative state-record files (apt/dpkg/jsonl), not just shell-replay logs.

**CLI:**

```
sovereign-osctl global-history recent [--since 24h] [--limit 50] [--source apt,dpkg,...]
sovereign-osctl global-history summary
sovereign-osctl global-history sources
sovereign-osctl global-history delta
sovereign-osctl global-history tail [--refresh 5] [--iterations N]    # R481 refresh-loop TUI
```

**Metric:** `sovereign_os_operator_global_history_query_total{verb,source,result}` (SDD-016 Layer B)

**Source:** R448 (E11.M5) shipped the verbs + 6-source aggregator. R481 (E11.M5+) shipped the refresh-loop tail TUI. R487 (E11.M5+) ships THIS dashboard, closing the surface-map waiver-slot 'dashboard: FUTURE — Grafana timeline panel'.

title: sovereign-os global-history
